answer:
\boxed{C}

Hemangioblastomas are highly vascular tumors commonly found in the brain (often in the cerebellum) and spinal cord in adults, typically presenting with symptoms like headaches, ataxia, or neurological deficits. While metastasis (A) can occur in the brain, it is not inherently highly vascular. Pilocytic astrocytomas (B) are less vascular and more common in children. Cavernous malformations (D) are vascular lesions but are not true tumors.
